_about: Simulated correction stream for the corrections-as-ground-truth eval (#759). Each entry is a recurring human correction: the off-brand term, what the team corrected it to, how many times it was seen (count), and a representative sentence in both its original (off-brand) and corrected (on-brand) form. The eval aggregates the stream into candidate rules, promotes those at or above min_count through the real loop (core/brand.ApplySuggestedRule — exactly what the server's PromoteAndSave does), then runs the brand-vocab check and asserts the promoted rule FLAGS each original (the mistake the team kept correcting) and does NOT flag the corrected fix. This measures the loop's core claim: a correction made repeatedly becomes a check that catches the mistake. Replace this simulated stream with an export of a real workspace's brand_voice_corrections to track the loop on live data.

corrections.original: Please utilize the dashboard to get started.
corrections.corrected: Please use the dashboard to get started.
corrections.note: above threshold → promoted; must catch the original

corrections.original: Teams can leverage the new API today.
corrections.corrected: Teams can use the new API today.

corrections.original: We value synergy across every team.
corrections.corrected: We value collaboration across every team.

corrections.original: We will onboard you in minutes.
corrections.corrected: We will set up your account in minutes.
corrections.note: at threshold → promoted; 'board' must not false-match (word boundary)
below threshold → NOT promoted; the check must stay silent until more evidence
